headline: On immigration, Spain moves against the tide of its European neighbors
subhead: Citing economic and moral arguments, Spanish Prime Minister Pedro Sanchez's Socialist government is preparing to grant legal status to 500,000 undocumented workers, most from Latin America. The plan brings hope to the 840,000 foreign nationals living without papers in Spain.

headline: Israel renews Lebanon strikes, forcing Syria border crossing to close
Trump appears to extend Iran deadline in cryptic post
Why is Donald Trump so mean?
subhead: By shamelessly flouting democratic norms and flaunting his amorality, brutality and bad faith, the American president restores pride to his humiliated electoral base, unsettles his opponents, and subverts the rules of the political game.

headline: Key facts to understand cadmium, an emerging public health issue
subhead: Cadmium, a heavy metal toxic to human health, is found in many everyday foods. Children and women are most affected by this carcinogenic contaminant, which is the subject of growing concern.

headline: Sex is an art form hidden in the shadows
subhead: Moral panic over the pleasures of the flesh prevents us from recognizing the artistic dimension of sexuality, as well as from passing down the best practices, argues Le Monde columnist  Maïa Mazaurette.

headline: War denies Lebanon's dead a final resting place
subhead: Forced to flee southern Lebanon due to Israeli air strikes and evacuation orders from the Israeli army, thousands of Lebanese have resigned themselves to burying loved ones far from their ancestral lands in makeshift graves.

'Retirees are no happier than working people'
subhead: It is simplistic to equate happiness with retirement, as retirement often appeals to those seeking to escape increasingly difficult working conditions, observes Le Monde's Béatrice Madeline in her column, drawing on a study by two economists.

headline: How 'The Camp of the Saints' became the far right's cult novel, from the Le Pens to MAGA
subhead: Since its publication in 1973, Jean Raspail's novel has shaped the imagination of ideologues who oppose immigration. The openly racist story, which envisions a million Indians descending on the French coast, was recently republished across the Atlantic.

headline: China's execution of a Frenchman convicted of drug trafficking casts a chill on relations with Paris
subhead: Chan Thao Phoumy was sentenced in 2010 after authorities dismantled a methamphetamine trafficking network. France has condemned the fact that his defense was not allowed to attend his final court hearing.
